title: tylecarpus andamanicus
intro: Tylecarpus andamanicus, also known as the Andaman Island tree, is a species of tree native to the Andaman and Nicobar Islands in India. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly the bark, which contains lignans and flavonoids with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The bark of Tylecarpus andamanicus has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, offering health benefits such as reducing inflammation and fighting oxidative stress. The bark can be prepared as a herbal decoction or extract, and it is also used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine to promote overall well-being and prevent various diseases.
